title: Decaspermum rubrum
taxonomy: Decaspermum rubrum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as the myrtle family. Members of the Myrtaceae family are native to various parts of the world, including Australia, Asia, and the Pacific. Within the Myrtaceae family, Decaspermum rubrum is further classified as belonging to the genus Decaspermum. The exact classification of Decaspermum rubrum is also supported by its morphological characteristics, such as its leaves and fruit.

intro: Decaspermum rubrum is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Australia and Southeast Asia, commonly known as the red-fruited forest berry or red forest berry. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory tract infections. The bioactive constituents of Decaspermum rubrum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
